zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel於1225年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾時，她的父親Thomas III de Bruyères25歲，母親Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais14歲。她於1237年與Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix在法國布格-福朗-可特艾列維斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1269年2月18日享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel于1225年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾时，她的父亲Thomas III de Bruyères25岁，母亲Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais14岁。她于1237年与Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix在法国布格-福朗-可特艾列維斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1269年2月18日享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bruyères
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Bruyère): from Old French bruiere ‘heather’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived in a place where heather grew, or a habitational name from one of the places in France (for example in Calvados) named with this word. Compare Labruyere .
Altered form (Bruyère) of French Bruguier: habitational name from (Le) Bruguier, the name of several places in the southeastern part of France, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Labruyere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jean-Philippe.

French (Labruyère): variant, with fused feminine definite article la, of Bruyère ‘heather’ (see Bruyere 1), a topographic name for someone who lived in a place where heather grew, or a habitational name from one of the places in France named La Bruyère.
Altered form (Labruyère) of French Labrière, a cognate of 1 above (see Labrier ).

variants.name: Briere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Pierre, Adrien, Aurele, Benedicte, Benoit, Colette, Fernand, Gaston, Marcel, Micheline.

French (Brière): topographic name from a regional variant of Old French bruiere ‘heather’ (see Bruyere ), or a habitational name from La Brière, the name of several places mainly in the northern part of France, from the same word.
History: Jean Brière from Clarbec in Calvados, France, married Jeanne Grandin in Quebec City, QC, in 1671.

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Chatel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celine, Emile, Gilles, Jacques, Lucien.
French (also Châtel):
habitational name from (Le) Chatel or (Le) Châtel, names of several places in various parts of France, from Old French chastel ‘castle, fortified building’.

variants.name: Chateau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Thierry, Francois, Michel.
French (Château):
from Old French chastel (French château) ‘castle, fortified building’, a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a castle, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in one.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Jean .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel was born in 1225, in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, her father, Thomas III de Bruyères, was 25 and her mother, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, was 14. She married Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix in 1237, in Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 18 February 1269, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel est née en 1225, à Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Thomas III de Bruyères, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix en 1237, à Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 février 1269, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel 1225 in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas III de Bruyères 25 und ihre Mutter Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais 14. Sie hat 1237 in Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 18. Februar 1269 im Alter von 44 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel è nata il nel 1225 nel Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Thomas III de Bruyères, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix nel 1237, nel Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 18 febbraio 1269, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatelが1225年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスエソンブルイeレス-レ-チaテルで生まれた時，父Thomas III de Bruyèresは25歳，母Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinaisは14歳であった。1237年にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeイオンレヴィスでGuy II de Lévis-Mirepoixと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1269年2月18日に44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel(이)가 1225년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 브루이e레스-레-cha텔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas III de Bruyères(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1237년 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 욘 레비스에서 Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1269년 2월 18일 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel nasceu em 1225, em Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Thomas III de Bruyères, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix em 1237, em Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 18 de fevereiro de 1269, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel родилась в 1225 г. в местности бруиeрес-ле-чaтел, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Thomas III de Bruyères было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix в 1237 г. в местности левис, енн, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 18 февраля 1269 г. в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de Bruyères Le Chatel nació en 1225, en Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Thomas III de Bruyères, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Agnès de Beaumont du Gatinais, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Guy II de Lévis-Mirepoix en 1237, en Levis, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 18 de febrero de 1269, a la edad de 44 años.
